evidence_quotes.quote: I spent real money on it and now I can't even play it legitimately.
they delete it even if we spent real money on it.
taken out content that were heavily relevant to the story

paragraphs.text: We need protection for products we buy, imagine if you bought a book and after ten years the original publisher of that book regardless of the authors opinion not only breaks into your house and takes the book but they burn it in front of you and tells you to buy the right to borrow the latest one which they can and will burn eventually. That is what it's like to buy digital games these days by big corporations that even if a game does well but in particular when it doesn't do well they delete it even if we spent real money on it. Some examples would be Gearboxes Battleborn, it was a live service game that could have been transformed into a fully functioning product outside servers as was proven by a few dedicated fans this year who revived the game. I spent real money on it and now I can't even play it legitimately. Games like Genshin Impact and Destiny 2 that are granted Free to play have taken out content that were heavily relevant to the story, so if you're a new player today you can't experience the same full game as a day 1 player did. Why do I a customer and a citizen of EU have to resort to piracy if I would want to experience some of these games at all since fans and pirates are the ones giving us real ownership of them while companies don't? Corporations have gone too far with their unchecked greed and anti customer decisions. It is time to set boundaries and laws that protect not only us customers but the workers aka the developers of said products to allow their creations to stay alive even to my grandchildrens time. Books and movies have protections to make sure they exists in the far future, games should too.' Thank you for taking the time to read my feedback.
